text:
Market today: Geopolitical tensions are heating up, pushing oil prices higher and raising inflation concerns. This creates a cautious mood, and tech/chip names, which are sensitive to growth and interest rates, could face headwinds. The Nasdaq-100 is already showing high volatility.

Your holdings: SOXL is a major concern, down over 23% and already below its stop; its leveraged nature and decay are actively hurting your book. SPAL, another leveraged ETF, is also down over 11% and nearing its stop. Margin amplifies these drops significantly.

Watch: Keep a close eye on oil price movements and their potential to fuel inflation fears. Also, monitor your leveraged positions (SOXL, SPAL) for any further weakness.

Bottom line: Tighten up risk management on your leveraged positions immediately to protect your capital.
plays: SOXL: Trim or sell SOXL immediately. It's a leveraged, decaying ETF, well below its stop at 200, and actively hurting your portfolio (-23.52%).
SPAL: Watch SPAL closely. It's a new, leveraged position down -11.17% and nearing its stop at 21.38. Respect that stop if it breaks.
No new buys today - hold and watch. Given the market uncertainty and issues with current leveraged holdings, adding new positions is not a priority.
